Ming Liu

mgliu

I am an Assistant Professor in the Computer Sciences Department at the University of Wisconsin-Madison since 2021. Iâm a member of the NetLab and madNets groups. I study networking and systems, with a focus on building efficient, robust, and practical networked systems. My work is driven by emerging hardware infrastructures, such as load-store interconnects, disaggregated storage, and programmable networks.

I am actively looking for students. If you are interested, feel free to email me.

Â 

Research

Recently, we build applications, systems, protocols, and utilities for composable rack-scale infrastructures.

  • Load-Store Interconnect
    • MemChannel develops a CSFQ-inspired transport layer for switched CXL memory pooling (NSDIâ26).
    • ChipletNet proposes Server Chiplet Networking and unearths its characteristics (HotNetsâ25).
    • PathFinder tracks and analyzes CXL.mem using Intel PMUs (SIGCOMMâ25).
    • MegaStation realizes the MIMO baseband processing on a single-node supercomputer (NSDIâ25).
    • rPCIeBench develops a benchmarking framework to characterize Routable PCIe (NSDIâ24).
  • Disaggregated Storage
    • SANarch compares the switched and switchless architecture for disaggregated SAN (NSDIâ26).
    • TapDB understands and optimizes database pushdown over storage disaggregation (ASPLOSâ26).
    • GluonFS ranked 26th in the 10-node category of IO500 and 33rd overall.
    • ntprof is a profiling utility for characterizing and analyzing the NVMe-over-TCP protocol (NSDIâ25).
    • Flint builds an elastic block storage over EBOFs using shadow views (NSDIâ25).
    • LEED develops a low-power and fast key-value store over SmartNIC JBOFs (SIGCOMMâ23).
  • Programmable Network
    • SG-IOV develops a socket-granular I/O virtualization approach to offload container networks (ASPLOSâ26).
    • SCR provides a framework enabling packet-granular software control over the harware transport (NSDIâ25).
    • RpcNIC is a software-hardware co-designed SmartNIC-native accelerator for the RPC stack (HPCAâ25).
    • LogNIC develops a high-level performance model for SmartNICs (MICROâ23).

Students

Zerui Guo, Wentao Hou, Yuyuan Kang, Joontaek Oh (postdoc)

Service

Program Committee Member: NSDI 2026, ASPLOS 2026, APNet 2025, SOSP 2024, HotInfra 2024, APNET 2024, OSDI 2024, NSDI 2024, HotInfra 2023, APNET 2023, SYSTOR 2023, SIGCOMM 2022, SYSTOR 2021
